description: Vector collection for Physical AI textbook content ingestion and retrieval

dense.description: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small embeddings for semantic search

quantization.comment: int8 quantization reduces storage 8x with <2% recall loss; uses disk offloading

sparse.description: BM25 sparse embeddings for keyword/hybrid search (optional, not required for MVP)

chapter_number.description: Chapter within module (1-12 across all modules)

heading_hierarchy.description: Breadcrumb: [Chapter X, Section Y, Subsection Z] for context

chunk_index.description: Sequential position in section (for ordering multi-chunk responses)

file_hash.description: SHA256 hash of source file for detecting updates

embedded_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when chunk was embedded

batch_id.description: OpenAI Batch API job ID for tracking bulk embeddings

description.m: Number of connections per point (16 = default; higher = more connections)
description.ef: Search parameter; higher = better recall but slower

hnsw_config.comment: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) achieves <30ms p95 latency at scale

indexing_strategy.comment: Payload stored on disk to reduce memory overhead

performance_targets.comment: Targets for monitoring/alerting on production instance

ingestion_config.batch_api_pricing: $0.02 per 1M tokens (75% savings vs. real-time API)
ingestion_config.comment: Use OpenAI Batch API for cost-effective bulk embedding

hybrid_search.comment: Combine semantic (dense) + keyword (sparse) search for better recall on technical terms

filter_capabilities.example_query: Search for 'bipedal locomotion' in Module 3, Chapters 8-9 excluding code examples

client_configuration_python.note: Use gRPC for lower latency; REST fallback available

migration_notes.from_pinecone: Qdrant provides lower latency + no lock-in vs. Pinecone
migration_notes.cost_comparison: Qdrant Cloud Free Tier sufficient for 100K vectors (~2.5GB); $0 cost
migration_notes.scaling: Qdrant scales to millions of vectors with minimal latency increase

quality_assurance.measurement_method: Evaluate on 100-200 representative queries against ground truth
quality_assurance.latency_monitoring: Track p50, p95, p99 latencies in production dashboards

example_payload.content: Bipedal locomotion refers to movement using two legs. In humanoid robots, this requires precise balance and coordination between the upper and lower body. The robot must continuously calculate the center of mass and adjust joint angles to maintain stability...
